descript.abstract: Actv_ProjectArea_SAIPlan represents an area (polygon) within which one or more Sale Area Improvement (SAI) related activities are aggregated or organized. The data comes from the Forest Service's Natural Resource Manager (NRM) Forest Activity Tracking System (FACTS), which is the agency standard for managing information about activities related to fire/fuels, silviculture, and invasive species. FACTS is an activity tracking application for all levels of the Forest Service.
descript.purpose: This dataset is a central source for project area boundaries for use in national information requests and cross unit analysis and makes the project area boundaries and their basic attributes more easily available to field units. It also provides public access to the data during project planning and implementation.
descript.supplinf: This feature class is part of a larger dataset of project areas which are described as NEPA (National Environmental Policy Act), Timber Sales, SAI Plans (Sale Area Improvement Plans), CFLRP (Collaborative Forest Landscape Restoration Program), Stewardship, and Other non-standard boundaries. These extents illustrate areas in which many activities occur that have a relationship. A boundary is, as itself, not an activity, it is the extent within which activities occur. For example, a project area for Timber Sale is the contractual area in which harvesting can take place. A NEPA boundary defines where the analysis takes place allowing future activities. An SAI Plan contains the area that KV (Knutson-Vandenberg) funded activities can take place. A Timber Sale designation is used for both the Timber Sale and SAI Plan if the SAI plan boundary is identical to the timber sale boundary. If the SAI plan is less than the Timber Sale boundary, both Timber Sale and SAI Plan boundaries are created. CFLRP boundaries encompass science-based ecosystem restoration of priority forest landscapes activities. Stewardship Boundaries define where stewardship funded activities take place.

idinfo.useconst: The USDA Forest Service makes no warranty, expressed or implied, including the warranties of merchantability and fitness for a particular purpose, nor assumes any legal liability or responsibility for the accuracy, reliability, completeness or utility of these geospatial data, or for the improper or incorrect use of these geospatial data. These geospatial data and related maps or graphics are not legal documents and are not intended to be used as such. The data and maps may not be used to determine title, ownership, legal descriptions or boundaries, legal jurisdiction, or restrictions that may be in place on either public or private land. Natural hazards may or may not be depicted on the data and maps, and users should exercise due caution. The data are dynamic and may change over time. The user is responsible to verify the limitations of the geospatial data and to use the data accordingly.

cntorgp.cntorg: USFS Chief Information Office, Enterprise Data Warehouse

cntinfo.cntvoice: Please send an e-mail to the address below.

idinfo.datacred: United States Forest Service Natural Resource Manager (NRM) Forest Activity Tracking System (FACTS)

dataqual.logic: FACTS Activity data is entered into the FACTS application by field units to describe planned or accomplished actions on the ground. The project areas encompassing these activities are also described in FACTS. Data entry is accomplished by authorized Forest Service personnel through a web application behind the U.S. Forest Service firewall and is restricted by access requirement protocols. Further data checks occur during data migration.
dataqual.complete: Data is accurate up to the publication date and attribute domains are valid values from Natural Resource Manager (NRM) Forest Activity Tracking System (FACTS).

horizpa.horizpar: Variable based on forest location – See Data_Source field for feature-specific accuracy.

procstep.procdesc: Data from an Oracle FACTS Sales Plan user view is joined to the spatial NRM FACTS Project Area polygons based on Unit ID, CN, and Name. Merged data is filtered to select project types SAIPlan before inserting data into EDW dataset. The process is run Sunday through Friday nights.

enttyp.enttypd: Feature class: A collection of geographic features with the same geometry type (such as point, line, or polygon), the same attributes, and the same spatial reference.

attr.attrdef: Internal feature number.

attrdomv.udom: Sequential unique whole numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: Globally unique identifier across all Forest Service databases, foreign key to most Forest Service National Application databases.
An identifier for this project - may be user-defined or system-assigned, depending on the ProjectType. This column duplicates data in Oracle, and may be overwritten by synchronization processes.
The name (if any) of the project. This column duplicates data in Oracle, and may be overwritten by synchronization processes.
Identifies the type of project. This column duplicates data in Oracle, and may be overwritten by synchronization processes.
Date of feature creation or latest update.
Feature data source code, provides information on how observation point or polygon features have been derived.

edom.edomvds: NRIS (Natural Resource Information System, a U.S. Forest Service database)

attr.attrdef: Distance (+/-), in dataset distance units, of expected difference between feature and real world object locations.
Indicates the type of trust fund plan: K-V for Knutson-Vandenberg or BD for Brush Disposal.
The sale category indicates the general type of sale. Historic, TIM Contract, Product Plan, or Multi Contract.

U.S. Forest Service Region, consisting of Region 01 (Northern Region), Region 02 (Rocky Mountain Region), Region 03 (Southwestern Region), Region 04 (Intermountain Region), Region 05 (Pacific Southwest Region), Region 06 (Pacific Northwest Region), Region 08 (Southern Region), Region 09 (Eastern Region), and Region 10 (Alaska Region). There is no Region 07. Regions are listed on https://www.fs.usda.gov/organization.
U.S. Forest Service Name. Regions are listed on https://www.fs.usda.gov/organization
The numerical code of the US Forest that administers the land.

attrdomv.udom: Forests are subdivisions of regions and can be found by drilling through regions on https://www.fs.usda.gov/organization

attr.attrdef: The name of the US Forest that administers the land.

attrdomv.udom: Forests are subdivisions of regions and can be found by drilling through regions on https://www.fs.usda.gov/organization

attr.attrdef: A code which uniquely identifies a Ranger District within a forest. For example, 01-17-01 is the Salmon River Ranger District in the Nez Perce-Clearwater National Forests in Region 01.
The name of a Ranger District within a forest. For example, 01-17-01 is the Salmon River Ranger District in the Nez Perce-Clearwater National Forests in Region 01.
Area of a feature in acres, calculated by converting Decimal Degrees to meters with the formula AreaMeters * 0.0002471054.

attrdomv.udom: The area of a feature in acres.

attr.attrdef: Feature geometry.
Area of feature in internal units squared.

attrdomv.udom: Positive real numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: Length of feature in internal units.

attrdomv.udom: Positive real numbers that are automatically generated.

The data are dynamic and may change over time. The user is responsible for verifying the limitations of the geospatial data and for using the data accordingly.
